elements.sent-2411-001: We solve an open problem posed in Thamrongthanyalak's paper on the definable Banach fixed point property.
elements.sent-2411-002: A Lipschitz curve selection is a key of our solution.
elements.sent-2411-003: In addition, we show a definable version of Caristi fixed point theorem.
elements.sent-2411-004: Throughout, $\mathcal F=(F,<,+,\cdot,0,1,\ldots)$ is a definably complete expansion of an ordered field.
elements.sent-2411-006: We call a locally o-minimal structure $\mathcal F$ o-minimal when we can choose $I=F$ \cite{D .}
elements.sent-2411-007: An open core $\mathcal F^\circ$ of $\mathcal F$ is the structure on $F$ generated by open sets definable in $\mathcal F$ \cite{DMS,Fornasiero_locally,MS .}
elements.sent-2411-008: In Thamrongthanyalak's paper \cite{Thamrongthanyalak , the Banach fixed point property (BFPP for short) is investigated.}
elements.sent-2411-009: A definable set $E$ has the BFPP if every definable contraction on $E$ has a fixed point.
elements.sent-2411-010: Every nonempty definable closed set enjoys BFPP by \cite[1.4]{Thamrongthanyalak .}
elements.sent-2411-011: Thamrongthanyalak showed that structures having o-minimal open core enjoy the strong BFPP and, if $\mathcal F$ possesses the BFPP, $\mathcal F$ has a locally o-minimal open core.
elements.sent-2411-012: The following question is posed in \cite{Thamrongthanyalak .}
elements.sent-2411-013: If $\mathcal F$ is definably complete and possesses the strong BFPP, is it o-minimal?.
elements.sent-2411-014: The following theorem answers the above question in the negative because non-o-minimal definably complete locally o-minimal expansion of an ordered field is already known \cite[Example 3.11]{Fornasiero_locally .}
elements.sent-2411-015: A definably complete locally o-minimal expansion of an ordered field possesses the strong BFPP.
elements.sent-2411-016: We prove a Lipschitz curve selection lemma in Section \ref{sec:curve_selection .}
elements.sent-2411-017: Theorem \ref{thm:local_case follows from the lemma in the same manner as \cite[Theorem A]{Thamrongthanyalak .}}
elements.sent-2411-018: A rough strategy of its proof is only given in the present paper.
elements.sent-2411-019: Theorem \ref{thm:local_case implies the `only if' part of the following corollary.}
elements.sent-2411-020: The `if' part was already proved as \cite[Theorem B]{Thamrongthanyalak .}
elements.sent-2411-021: A definably complete expansion of an ordered field has a locally o-minimal open core if and only if it possesses the BFPP.
elements.sent-2411-022: The Caristi fixed point theorem is a generalization of the Banach fixed point theorem \cite{Caristi .}
elements.sent-2411-023: A metric space has the Caristi fixed point property if and only if it is complete \cite{Weston .}
elements.sent-2411-024: We prove a similar equivalence holds in definably complete structures in Section \ref{sec:caristi .}
elements.sent-2411-025: For every definable lower semi-continuous function $f:X \to [0,\infty)$, there exists $x_0 \in X$ such that $S_f(x_0)=\{x_0\}$, where $S_f(x) :=\{y \in X\;|\;\|x-y\| \leq f(x)-f(y)\}$ for every $x \in X$.
elements.sent-2411-026: Thamrongthanyalak used Fischer's $\Lambda^m$-regular stratification \cite{Fischer in his paper \cite{Thamrongthanyalak .}}
elements.sent-2411-027: In locally o-minimal structures, such a stratification is not always available.
elements.sent-2411-028: For our purpose, a weaker substitute called Lipschitz curve selection (Lemma \ref{lem:Lipschitz ) is enough.}
elements.sent-2411-030: The parameterized family $\{S_t\;|\;t \in T\}$ of definable subsets of $X$ is called definable if the union $\bigcup_{t \in T \{t\} \times S_t$ is definable.}
elements.sent-2411-031: For a set $X$, a definable family $\mathcal C$ of subsets of $X$ is called a definable filtered collection if, for any $B_1, B_2 \in \mathcal C$, there exists $B_3 \in \mathcal C$ with $B_3 \subseteq B_1 \cap B_2$.
elements.sent-2411-032: We say that $X$ is definably compact if, for every definable filtered collection $\mathcal C$ of nonempty closed subsets of $X$, $\bigcap_{C \in\mathcal C C$ is non-empty.}
elements.sent-2411-033: A definable subset $X$ of $F^n$ is bounded and closed if and only if it is definably compact.
elements.sent-2411-034: See \cite[Proposition 3.10]{J , which proves the lemma when the structure is o-minimal; the same proof works when the structure is definably complete.}
elements.sent-2411-035: We introduce the notations used in the proof of Lemma \ref{lem:Lipschitz .}
